title: 0He0Ethr650Zwmdmrunqtg4Ur0E6Cd
summary:
"Amidst this tumultuous backdrop, Baltic Dry Indices concluded this week on a flattish tone. Neither the banking industry turbulence of late nor the modest fresh Chinese stimulus was able to have a bearing on their mood, at least up to now."
By Michalis Voutsinas
In an eventful week, the Baltic Dry Index managed to stay on an upward trajectory, concluding today at 1535 points. The last couple of trading days, the spot market of the gearless segments was rather lukewarm, dragging the general index down from its intra-week, current year highs of 1603 points.
